content.full_text:
February 8, 1991

Blanca Breeze
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Breeze:

This letter is to follow up our telephone conversation of February 8. As I mentioned, a letter of advice from you would be appreciated.

I serve as a Planning Commissioner for the city of Huntington Beach. I also serve as Executive Director of the Bolsa Chica Conservancy, a non-profit corporation conducting environmental research and education programs. This is a part-time, paid position. Since the Conservancy does not own land or engage in any commercial activities, its activities are not likely to involve Planning Commission decision-making.

If the Conservancy were ever to have business before the Planning Commission, should I disqualify myself due to conflict of interest?

Local firms and individuals have donated financially to the Conservancy. Such contributions indirectly benefit me financially. One land development firm, the Koll Company, is the primary land owner in the Bolsa Chica area. While I am not paid directly by the Koll Company, they have made significant financial contributions to the Conservancy, and a Koll Company employee sits on the Conservancy's Board of Directors. Koll will certainly have items before the Planning Commission during my term of office.

Should I disqualify myself from consideration of applications by the Koll Company?

Should I disqualify myself from consideration of applications by firms or individuals who have made smaller financial contributions to the Conservancy?

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

G. Victor Leipzig, Ph.D.
Executive Director
